name: Receive and Send Messages Across WhatsApp, Instagram and Facebook Messenger with Fiwano

parameters.content:
## Receive → Any Logic → Send
### WhatsApp · Instagram DM · Facebook Messenger

**One Trigger/Action node set for three channels.** Fiwano normalizes messages received over the official Meta APIs, so the same n8n fields and nodes work across all three channels.

This workflow is a minimal integration example:

`Fiwano Trigger → your logic → Fiwano Send`

The included text and attachment branches only demonstrate receiving, reading the normalized payload, and sending a reply. Replace the middle with any routing, CRM, helpdesk, business-rule, database, or AI logic.

### Before you start
Connect at least one channel through the [Fiwano portal](https://fiwano.com) or Fiwano's channel-connection API. The authorization flow simplifies connecting your own or client channels without creating and reviewing a separate Meta app for the workflow.

### Setup
1. Install the verified **n8n-nodes-fiwano** community package from the n8n editor.
2. Add your **Fiwano API** credential to the Trigger and both example send nodes.
3. *(Recommended)* Set a **Webhook Secret** on that credential so the Trigger can verify incoming signatures.
4. **Save and activate** the workflow.

The Trigger uses **Auto-Setup → All Active Channels** to register this workflow for every active channel. Re-activate after connecting another channel. Keep **Event Types** on *Message Received*. n8n must be publicly reachable.

[Fiwano n8n documentation](https://fiwano.com/documentation/n8n)

### Same inbound shape for all three channels

Read these fields from `$json` on the trigger:

- `channel_id` — connected channel; pass it to the send node
- `channel_type` — `whatsapp`, `instagram`, or `messenger`
- `data.from` — sender; pass it as the reply recipient
- `data.from_name` — sender name when available
- `data.type` — `text`, `image`, `audio`, `video`, `document`…
- `data.text` — message text when `type = text`
- `data.media` — media metadata for attachments

You normally do not need to branch by `channel_type`. Use it only when your business logic or channel-specific UI requires it.
